task_completion.description: Verify that the messy invoices and vendor master data were merged and loaded into the model.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Data/Fields pane on the right side of Power BI. You should see columns from both sources, specifically 'Vendor_Canonical', 'Category', and 'Amount' available in the same table or linked tables.

task_completion.description: Verify that the DAX measure 'Spend_Share_Pct' was created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'Spend_Share_Pct' in the Data/Fields pane (it should have a small calculator icon next to it), or look for the DAX formula bar showing the calculation for 'Spend_Share_Pct'.

task_completion.description: Verify that a table visual was created displaying the required columns.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the main report canvas for a Table visual. It must contain the columns: 'Vendor_Canonical', 'Category', 'Amount', and 'Spend_Share_Pct'. Check that 'Omega Marketing Group' shows an Amount of 4000.

task_completion.description: Verify that the visual data was exported to consolidated_spend.csv on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file named 'consolidated_spend.csv' on the Desktop in the File Explorer, or a 'Save As' dialog showing the export of the visual data to this path.

task_completion.description: Verify that the Power BI report was saved as Vendor_Spend_Analysis.pbix.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the title bar of the Power BI application; it should read 'Vendor_Spend_Analysis - Power BI Desktop'. Alternatively, look for 'Vendor_Spend_Analysis.pbix' on the Desktop in the File Explorer.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Power BI Desktop application to perform the tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Power BI Desktop interface (ribbon, canvas, panes) being actively used, rather than a terminal or Python script generating the files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used Power Query to merge the data rather than manually typing a static table.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots of the Power Query Editor showing the 'Merge Queries' dialog, 'Fuzzy matching' options checked, or the 'Applied Steps' pane showing the merge.

integrity.description: Verify the DAX measure is a real calculation and not hardcoded text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The formula bar should show a valid DAX calculation (e.g., using DIVIDE, SUM, CALCULATE, ALL) for Spend_Share_Pct, not just static numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 5 distinct vendors with a total spend of 12000. If the fuzzy merge is performed correctly, the total spend for 'Omega Marketing Group' must be exactly 4000. The expected output files are 'consolidated_spend.csv' and 'Vendor_Spend_Analysis.pbix' located on the Desktop.
